ucs.title: [LLM] APT35 (Charming Kitten / Mint Sandstorm) CharmPower / POWERSTAR LNK-in-archive PowerShell stager
ucs.description: Hunts the signature APT35 / TA453 initial-access chain: a password-protected ZIP/RAR delivered via rapport-building spear-phish is extracted to disk, the user double-clicks an LNK that pivots through explorer.exe to spawn powershell.exe with -WindowStyle Hidden + a Net.WebClient/Invoke-Expression download cradle for the CharmPower / POWERSTAR / GhostEcho stage-2. The combination of archive-utility ancestry + hidden PowerShell + download cradle is highly specific to Charming Kitten lures (Microsoft Mint Sandstorm reporting, Volexity POWERSTAR).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("explorer.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe","winrar.exe","winrar64.exe","winzip32.exe","winzip64.exe","wzzip.exe") OR Processes.parent_process IN ("*.lnk*")) Processes.user!="*$" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(process,"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?") OR match(process,"(?i)\s-(nop|noprofile|noni|noninter)\b") | where match(process,"(?i)(DownloadString|Invoke-WebRequest|\bIEX\b|Invoke-Expression|FromBase64String|Net\.WebClient|wget\s|curl\s)") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT35 / Mint Sandstorm — LNK-in-archive PowerShell stager (CharmPower / POWERSTAR pattern)
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
// Parent indicates archive-extracted execution: archive util (open-archive double click) or explorer.exe (LNK double click)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (
    "explorer.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe",
    "winrar.exe","winrar64.exe",
    "winzip32.exe","winzip64.exe","wzzip.exe")
// Charming Kitten lures consistently use hidden window + bypassed profile
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
     or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(nop|noprofile|noni|noninter)\b"
// Stage-1 download cradle — POWERSTAR / CharmPower fetch their main module from compromised WP / Dropbox / OneDrive
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (
    "DownloadString","Invoke-WebRequest","IEX","Invoke-Expression",
    "FromBase64String","Net.WebClient","wget ","curl ")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256, IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT35 / Mint Sandstorm — LNK-in-archive PowerShell stager (CharmPower / POWERSTAR pattern)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe"
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\explorer.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\7zfm.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\7zg.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\winrar.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\winrar64.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\winzip32.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\winzip64.exe"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
     or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(nop|noprofile|noni|noninter)\b"
| where CommandLine has_any (
    "DownloadString","Invoke-WebRequest","IEX","Invoke-Expression",
    "FromBase64String","Net.WebClient","wget ","curl ")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT35 Charming Kitten LNK-in-Archive PowerShell Stager
id: 9c9d4e72-8f3e-4a1d-9d5a-9f0b7c5b1a01
status: experimental
description: Detects the APT35 / Mint Sandstorm CharmPower / POWERSTAR initial access pattern - PowerShell launched from explorer.exe or an archive utility with hidden window and a network download cradle.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/mint-sandstorm-iranian-threat-actor
  - https://www.volexity.com/blog/2023/06/iran-charming-kitten-powerstar/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1566
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.g0059
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\7zfm.exe'
      - '\7zg.exe'
      - '\winrar.exe'
      - '\winrar64.exe'
      - '\winzip32.exe'
      - '\winzip64.exe'
  selection_hidden:
    CommandLine|re|i: '\s-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?|\s-(nop|noprofile|noni|noninter)\b'
  selection_cradle:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'Invoke-WebRequest'
      - 'IEX'
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - 'Net.WebClient'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_image and selection_parent and selection_hidden and selection_cradle and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IT scripts launched manually from a desktop shortcut (rare; should be allowlisted by signed cert / path)
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT35's documented stage-1 (CharmPower 2022, POWERSTAR/GhostEcho 2023, BellaCiao 2023) consistently delivers a password-protected archive containing a malicious LNK whose target invokes powershell.exe -windowstyle hidden -nop with a download cradle — the parent is therefore explorer.exe (LNK launch) or the archive utility (extracted then directly run). Generic 'PowerShell with download cradle' det

ucs.title: [LLM] APT35 (TA453) post-compromise mailbox surveillance — inbox rule creation following risky AAD sign-in
ucs.description: Hunts the APT35 / TA453 / Charming Kitten post-takeover playbook documented by PwC, Mandiant, and Microsoft: after credential capture (typically via Evilginx-style fake login or OAuth abuse), the operator signs in and within minutes creates an inbox rule that auto-forwards / hides / moves messages containing keywords like 'password', 'invoice', 'security', 'login' — preserving access to victim mail without needing repeated logons. The detection chains a risky/anonymous-IP sign-in to a same-user inbox-rule creation within 60 minutes — a pairing characteristic of TA453 (COBALT ILLUSION) ops agai
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Change where nodename=All_Changes All_Changes.object_category=mailbox All_Changes.command IN ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules","Add-MailboxPermission") by All_Changes.user All_Changes.src All_Changes.command All_Changes.object _time | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Changes)` | rename _time as ruleTime | where match(object,"(?i)(ForwardTo|RedirectTo|DeleteMessage|MoveToFolder|MarkAsRead|SubjectContainsWords|BodyContainsWords|password|invoice|security|login|verification|2fa|otp)") | join type=inner user [ | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as signinTime from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.app IN ("AzureActiveDirectory","Office365","AzureAD") Authentication.action=success (Authentication.signature_id=*risk* OR Authentication.tag=anonymous OR Authentication.src_country!="US") by Authentication.user Authentication.src Authentication.src_country | rename Authentication.user as user Authentication.src as signinSrc Authentication.src_country as signinCountry ] | where ruleTime >= signinTime AND (ruleTime - signinTime) <= 3600 | table ruleTime user src signinSrc signinCountry command object
ucs.kql:
// APT35 / TA453 — post-compromise inbox rule following risky AAD sign-in (mailbox surveillance setup)
let WindowMin = 60m;
let RiskySignins = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | where RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ("medium","high")
         or RiskLevelAggregated in ("medium","high")
         or IsAnonymousProxy == true
         or RiskState =~ "atRisk"
    | project SigninTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, AccountObjectId,
              SigninIP = IPAddress, SigninCountry = Country,
              RiskLevelDuringSignIn, ClientAppUsed;
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where Application in~ ("Microsoft Exchange Online","Office 365")
| where ActionType in~ ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules",
                          "Add-MailboxPermission","Set-Mailbox")
| extend RawJson = tostring(RawEventData)
// TA453 rule shapes: forwarding / hiding / keyword filtering on credentials & finance
| where RawJson has_any ("ForwardTo","RedirectTo","DeleteMessage","MoveToFolder",
                          "MarkAsRead","SubjectContainsWords","BodyContainsWords",
                          "password","invoice","security","login",
                          "verification","2fa","otp","hacked")
| join kind=inner RiskySignins on $left.AccountObjectId == $right.AccountObjectId
| where Timestamp between (SigninTime .. SigninTime + WindowMin)
| project RuleTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, AccountObjectId,
          ActionType, ActorIP = IPAddress, ActorCountry = CountryCode,
          SigninTime, SigninIP, SigninCountry, RiskLevelDuringSignIn,
          ClientAppUsed, RuleParams = RawJson
| order by RuleTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT35 / TA453 — post-compromise inbox rule following risky AAD sign-in (mailbox surveillance setup)
let WindowMin = 60m;
let RiskySignins = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where ResultType == 0
    | where RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ("medium","high")
         or RiskLevelAggregated in ("medium","high")
         or RiskState =~ "atRisk"
    | extend SigninCountry = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion)
    | project SigninTime = TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, UserId,
              SigninIP = IPAddress, SigninCountry,
              RiskLevelDuringSignIn, ClientAppUsed;
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OfficeWorkload =~ "Exchange"
| where Operation in~ ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules",
                         "Add-MailboxPermission","Set-Mailbox")
| where ResultStatus =~ "Succeeded" or ResultStatus =~ "True"
| extend Params = tostring(Parameters)
// TA453 rule shapes: forwarding / hiding / keyword filtering on credentials & finance
| where Params has_any ("ForwardTo","RedirectTo","DeleteMessage","MoveToFolder",
                          "MarkAsRead","SubjectContainsWords","BodyContainsWords",
                          "password","invoice","security","login",
                          "verification","2fa","otp","hacked")
| join kind=inner RiskySignins on $left.UserId == $right.UserPrincipalName
| where TimeGenerated between (SigninTime .. SigninTime + WindowMin)
| project RuleTime = TimeGenerated, UserId, ClientIP, Operation, Params,
          SigninTime, SigninIP, SigninCountry, RiskLevelDuringSignIn, ClientAppUsed
| order by RuleTime desc
ucs.rationale: TA453 / Charming Kitten's tradecraft post-credential-capture is well-documented (PwC 2022 'Yellow Liderc', Mandiant 2023, Microsoft Threat Intelligence): the operator immediately establishes mailbox surveillance via Outlook/OWA inbox rules so they keep collecting after MFA refresh or password reset. Generic 'new inbox rule' alerts are noisy because users legitimately auto-file/forward; tightening
